fault_hypothesis: The condition on line 70 is inverted. The clamping logic is applied when `clamp` is `False` and skipped when `clamp` is `True`, which is the opposite of the intended behavior.

constraints.intent: Failures occur only when extrapolating, i.e., when the interpolation parameter t is outside the [0, 1] range.

spec.expr: (clamp and (min(y0, y1) <= y__3 <= max(y0, y1))) or ((not clamp) and abs(y__3 - y__1) < 1e-9)

constraints.intent: The value of y should be clamped if and only if the clamp parameter is True.
When clamping is requested, the interpolated value must be within the clamping range for the result to be correct without explicit clamping.
